_README: Per-repo settings for is-it-live. TWO ways to configure (pick one):
1. PREFERRED: drop a .is-it-live.json in your repo root (portable,
travels with the repo, works in worktrees). Run `is_it_live.py --init`
inside the repo to scaffold one.
2. Central: copy this file to config.json (gitignored, stays on your
machine) and key entries by absolute repo path, like the example below.
All fields are optional. With no config at all the tool still shows the
full local ladder up to MERGED — and if your repo is on GitHub with `gh`
installed, deployment records can prove LIVE with zero config.
Field guide:
provider              vercel|netlify|fly|render|heroku|vm|custom|auto
production_branch     the branch production deploys from (default: auto-detect)
production_urls       URLs real users hit; probed for reachability
version_sources       BEST upgrade: endpoints that echo the deployed commit
SHA. One entry per deploy surface (frontend, backend...).
'json_path' picks a field when the endpoint returns JSON.
health_url            a health endpoint, shown for context
bundle_regex          regex for a fingerprintable build asset in the HTML
deploy_workflows      GitHub Actions workflow names whose successful runs
prove a deploy (default: any workflow named *deploy*)
ignore_branch_prefixes branch prefixes to hide from the 'notable unmerged' list
components            the project-specific visual layer: named parts of the
codebase (paths = dir prefixes or globs). Each shows up
as a card: where its newest change sits on the ladder,
dirty files, recent activity. importance is a free-text
badge (e.g. core).
notes                 one evergreen line about what production IS; date any
temporal claim, e.g. 'as of 2026-06-01: ...'

/absolute/path/to/your-repo.notes: One evergreen line about what production is.
